data.description: Singapore sees $6.2B private real estate fund from Hongkong Land

data.title: Anthony Scaramucci praises JPMorgan for issuing $50 million in U.S. commercial paper on Solana blockchain, supporting his investment thesis.
data.description: SkyBridge Capital's founder, Anthony Scaramucci, expressed enthusiasm on Thursday regarding JPMorgan Chase & Co.'s decision to issue debt securities through Solana. This move aligns with Scaramucci's investment perspective. JPMorgan's issuance of $50 million in U.S. commercial paper for Galaxy Digital Inc. directly on Solana marks a significant event as a major global bank embraces a public blockchain for security issuance. Interestingly, this initiative follows JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon's recent statement about exploring blockchain technology for client service enhancements. Scaramucci, a prominent cryptocurrency supporter, welcomed this development as a positive indicator for his investment strategy focused on Solana.

data.title: Binance aligns with Trump's crypto platform, incorporating USD1 stablecoin pairs for zero-fee trading with backing support.
data.description: Binance recently introduced several trading pairs with zero fees for the Trump family's stablecoin. This move is part of Binance's strategy to enhance its stablecoin collateral by utilizing the Trump family's stablecoin. The initiative aims to improve trading efficiency and reduce costs for users.
Markets react to Fed easing; gold prices soar

data.title: T1 Energy prices public offerings of $140M in convertible notes due 2030 & 28.28M shares of common stock at $4.95/share. Upsized from $120M previously announced.
data.description: On December 11, 2025, T1 Energy Inc. (NYSE: TE) based in Austin, Texas and New York, disclosed the pricing details of their recent public offerings. The offerings included $140.0 million aggregate principal amount of 5.25% convertible senior notes due 2030 (referred to as the “Convertible Notes Offering”) and 28,282,830 shares of common stock priced at $4.95 per share (known as the “Common Stock Offering”). The Convertible Notes Offering expanded from the initial $120.0 million aggregate principal amount.
Thai official: Acting PM scheduled for call with Trump Friday evening
SETI climbs 0.4% to 1,258.51 points at market open in Thailand

data.title: Popping candy company in China shifts to "Intelligent Manufacturing" as global OEM/ODM strategy. #Globalization #Baida
data.description: In Chaozhou, China, on December 11, 2025, it was reported that with the advancement of globalization, the label of "Made in China" is shifting towards "Intelligent Manufacturing in China." Baida Popping Candy, a prominent player in the global popping candy industry, is at the forefront of this transformation. The company is actively incorporating smart technologies into its production processes to enhance efficiency.

data.title: China has transformed from poverty to a tech and economic powerhouse in less than 50 years. Leaders interpret the country's growth.
data.description: In just under 50 years, China has transformed from one of the globe's poorest nations to a leading force in technology and economics. The country has accomplished remarkable development feats. Chinese leaders offer insights on how they perceive the nation's growth...

data.title: Spotlight Energy acquires PathPoint Energy, expanding operations in natural gas trading and logistics in Houston.
data.description: Houston-based company Spotlight Energy has successfully completed the acquisition of PathPoint Energy. This strategic move has led to the expansion of its natural gas trading, logistics, and asset optimization platform. The acquisition is expected to significantly increase the company's market presence and scale, with the transaction amounting to approximately $X. Spotlight Energy, operating under the hashtag OnePWRSolutions, continues to strengthen its position in the energy sector through strategic acquisitions and operational enhancements.

data.title: Stock market surges with Dow Jones hitting record highs, Russell 2000 surges, and Nasdaq cutting losses. Broadcom AI chip sales double.
data.description: Both the Dow Jones and Russell 2000 indices achieved all-time highs, with the Nasdaq recovering from initial declines. Broadcom projected a 100% increase in sales of AI chips. Despite this positive outlook, Broadcom's stock dropped. Dow Jones Futures are on the rise amid these developments.

No tsunami damage expected following Japan earthquake - NHK

data.title: SEC approves DTCC to tokenize securities including stocks, ETFs, and US Treasurys in 2022 after granting a no-action letter.
data.description: The Depository Trust and Clearing Corporation (DTCC) is making strides in the blockchain space by preparing to tokenize a variety of assets including stocks, ETFs, and US Treasurys in the upcoming year. This follows the reception of a no-action letter from the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) approving their initiative. This move signifies a significant shift in the traditional financial sector towards embracing digital assets, with DTCC leading the charge in this evolution.

data.title: New order from Trump admin triggers DOJ challenge to state AI laws, focusing on Colorado's discrimination statute.
data.description: The DOJ has been directed by the White House to challenge state laws related to artificial intelligence. Specifically, the focus is on Colorado's statute addressing algorithmic discrimination. This move comes amidst growing concerns regarding the regulation of AI technologies at the state level. The exact number of states with similar laws and the potential impact of this directive on the legal landscape of AI governance remain unclear.

data.title: Arkansas TV chooses to drop PBS due to lack of federal funds, facing a tough call to stay in operation.
data.description: The executive director of an Arkansas TV station revealed that due to the loss of federal funding, they faced a challenging choice: either discontinue PBS programming or shut down completely.
